description: HCT Library sample knowledge base — powers demo mode. Replace or supplement with real documents via the ingestion pipeline.

entries.q: What are the library opening hours?
entries.a:
HCT Library is open Monday through Thursday from 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM. The library is closed on Fridays, Saturdays, and UAE public holidays. During examination periods, extended hours may be offered — check the library website for announcements: https://hct.ac.ae/en/libraries/about-hct-library/library-staff-hours/

For after-hours access to e-resources, all databases are available 24/7 through the library portal.

entries.q: Is the library open during Ramadan?
entries.a:
During Ramadan, HCT Library typically operates reduced hours: Sunday through Thursday from 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM. Please check the library website or contact us at library@hct.ac.ae for the exact schedule, as hours may vary by campus.

Current hours: https://hct.ac.ae/en/libraries/about-hct-library/library-staff-hours/

entries.q: How do I access library databases from home?
entries.a:
HCT Library provides remote access to all electronic databases 24/7. To access databases off-campus:
1. Go to the databases page: https://hct.ac.ae/en/libraries/e-resources/all-databases/
2. Select your database (e.g., EBSCO, ProQuest, JSTOR)
3. When prompted, enter your HCT student or staff ID and password
If you experience login issues, contact the Reference Desk at library@hct.ac.ae.

entries.q: What databases does HCT Library provide?
entries.a:
HCT Library provides access to over 50 electronic databases across all subject areas. Key resources include:
• EBSCO Academic Search Complete — multidisciplinary journals
• ProQuest Central — full-text academic content
• JSTOR — humanities and social sciences archives
• IEEE Xplore — engineering and technology
• ScienceDirect — science and medicine (Elsevier)
• Business Source Elite — business and management
• CINAHL — nursing and allied health
• PsycINFO — psychology and behavioural science
• Web of Science — citation tracking and research analytics
For a full list, visit: https://hct.ac.ae/en/libraries/e-resources/all-databases/

entries.q: How do I search for peer-reviewed articles?
entries.a:
To find peer-reviewed articles:
1. Go to a database such as EBSCO Academic Search Complete
2. Enter your topic keywords in the search box
3. Apply the 'Peer-Reviewed' or 'Scholarly Journals' filter (usually a checkbox on the left)
4. Add date filters to limit to recent publications if needed
5. Use AND/OR/NOT operators to refine: e.g., 'climate change AND UAE AND policy'
Tip: If you're not finding enough results, try broader keywords or check additional databases. A subject librarian can assist with advanced search strategies.

entries.q: How do I cite a journal article in APA 7th edition?
entries.a:
APA 7th edition journal article format:

Author, A. A., & Author, B. B. (Year). Title of article. Journal Name, Volume(Issue), Page–Page. https://doi.org/xxxxx

Example:
Al Hamli, M., & Smith, J. (2023). Digital transformation in UAE higher education. Journal of Higher Education Technology, 15(2), 45–62. https://doi.org/10.1234/jhet.2023.45

Key rules:
• Up to 20 authors: list all; 21+: list first 19, ..., then last author
• Italicise the journal name and volume number
• Include DOI where available
• No 'Retrieved from' needed for stable online sources
For more help, visit the APA 7th edition guide on the library website.

entries.q: How do I cite a website in APA?
entries.a:
APA 7th edition website citation:

Author, A. A. (Year, Month Day). Title of page. Site Name. URL

Example:
HCT Library. (2024, March 15). Research guide: Engineering. Higher Colleges of Technology. https://library.hct.ac.ae/guides/engineering

If no author: Start with the title
If no date: Use (n.d.) instead of year
If content may change: Add 'Retrieved Month Day, Year, from URL'

For official reports and government sites, treat the organisation as the author.

entries.q: How do I cite a book in APA 7th edition?
entries.a:
APA 7th edition book format:

Author, A. A. (Year). Title of book: Subtitle if any (Edition if not first). Publisher. https://doi.org/xxxxx (if ebook)

Example:
Kotler, P., & Armstrong, G. (2021). Principles of marketing (18th ed.). Pearson.

Edited book:
Edwards, R. (Ed.). (2022). Advances in library technology. Academic Press.

Chapter in edited book:
Brown, T. (2022). AI in reference services. In R. Edwards (Ed.), Advances in library technology (pp. 45–72). Academic Press.

entries.q: How many books can I borrow?
entries.a:
HCT Library borrowing limits by user category:
• Undergraduate students: 5 books, 14-day loan period
• Postgraduate students: 8 books, 21-day loan period
• Academic staff: 15 books, 30-day loan period
• Administrative staff: 5 books, 14-day loan period

Renewals: Items can be renewed online through your library account, by phone, or in person — unless another user has placed a hold on the item. Maximum 2 renewals per item.

entries.q: What are the library fines for overdue books?
entries.a:
Overdue fines at HCT Library:
• Standard loan items: AED 0.50 per day per item
• Reserve/high-demand items: AED 1.00 per day per item
• Maximum fine per item: AED 50.00

Fines can be paid at the library circulation desk. Users with outstanding fines of AED 25 or more will have borrowing privileges suspended until the balance is cleared. Lost items: replacement cost plus a AED 10 processing fee.

entries.q: How do I renew my library books?
entries.a:
You can renew library items in three ways:
1. Online: Log into your library account via the library portal and click 'Renew'. Borrowing details: https://hct.ac.ae/en/libraries/supporting-you/borrowing-privileges/
2. In person: Bring the item to the circulation desk
3. By phone: Call the circulation desk during library hours

Note: Items cannot be renewed if another user has placed a hold, or if you have reached the maximum renewal limit (2 renewals per item).

entries.q: How do I book a study room?
entries.a:
HCT Library offers group study rooms for 2–8 people. To book:
1. Go to the room booking system: https://hct.libcal.com/
2. Select your preferred date, time, and room size
3. Log in with your HCT ID
4. Choose a time slot (maximum 2 hours per booking per day)
5. You will receive an email confirmation

Rooms are available during library operating hours. Bookings can be made up to 7 days in advance. Cancellations must be made at least 2 hours before the booking start time.

For help with room booking, contact LTS tech lrstech@hct.ac.ae

entries.q: What equipment is available in study rooms?
entries.a:
HCT Library study rooms are equipped with:
• Large display screen with HDMI/wireless connectivity
• Whiteboard with markers
• High-speed Wi-Fi
• Power outlets for all devices
• Air conditioning

Some rooms also have:
• Video conferencing equipment (select rooms)
• 3D printer access (Innovation Lab)
• Recording studio facilities (Media Room)

Check the room booking system for specific equipment available in each room.

entries.q: How do I print or scan in the library?
entries.a:
Printing and scanning services at HCT Library:

Printing:
• Black & white: AED 0.20 per page
• Colour: AED 0.50 per page
• Upload your document via a library computer or the print release station
• Release your print job at any printer using your HCT ID card

Scanning:
• Free for all HCT students and staff
• Scan to email or USB drive
• Scanning stations located near the main reference area

Large format printing and binding services are available — ask at the circulation desk for details.

entries.q: How do I get help with my research?
entries.a:
HCT Library offers several research support services:

1. Ask a Librarian: Email library@hct.ac.ae or use the live chat on the library website for quick questions
2. Reference Desk: Visit in person for one-on-one research assistance — no appointment needed
3. Research Consultation: Book a 30-minute appointment with a subject librarian for in-depth research strategy guidance
4. Subject Guides: Pre-built research guides for each subject area at https://libguides.hct.ac.ae/?b=s
5. Library Workshops: Regular information literacy sessions — check the library website for the events calendar

For thesis and dissertation research support, contact the academic support team.

entries.q: What is interlibrary loan and how do I request it?
entries.a:
Interlibrary Loan (ILL) allows you to borrow books and articles not held in HCT Library's collection from other libraries.

How to request a book (ILL):
1. Search the HCT Library catalogue first to confirm the item is not available
2. Submit your ILL request at: https://hct.ac.ae/en/libraries/supporting-you/interlibrary-loan/
3. Provide full citation details: author, title, publisher, year, ISBN/ISSN
4. Typical delivery time: 3–10 working days

How to request an article:
• Use the article request form: https://myhctsupport.hct.ac.ae/HEAT/Modules/SelfService/?NoDefaultProvider=True#serviceCatalog/request/51A8DEA49F664FF79D7009BB3450371E

ILL is free for HCT students and staff. Books are loaned for the standard loan period. Digital articles are delivered as PDF files to your email.

entries.q: Does the library have resources for my academic research or capstone project?
entries.a:
HCT Library provides comprehensive resources support:

1. Academic Writing Guides: Available at https://libguides.hct.ac.ae/?b=s
2. Citation Management Tools: RefWorks and Zotero — free for all HCT students (ask for training)
3. APA 7th Edition Guide: Detailed referencing guide with examples
4. Literature Search: Book a consultation with a subject librarian for systematic search strategy
5. Databases: Scopus and Scival for citation analysis and gap identification
6. 

For detailed resources support, book a research consultation with your subject librarian.

entries.q: How do I use Zotero for citation management?
entries.a:
Zotero is a free citation management tool available to all HCT students and staff.

Getting started:
1. Download Zotero at https://zotero.org (free)
2. Install the browser connector (Chrome/Firefox) to save references directly from databases
3. Organise references into collections by project or subject
4. Use the Word/Google Docs plugin to insert citations as you write
5. Generate a reference list automatically in APA 7th edition format

HCT Library offers Zotero training workshops — check the events calendar. One-on-one help available at the Reference Desk.

entries.q: How do I evaluate a source for academic use?
entries.a:
Use the CRAAP Test to evaluate any source:

C — Currency: When was it published? Is it recent enough for your topic?
R — Relevance: Does it directly address your research question?
A — Authority: Who is the author? What are their credentials? Is it peer-reviewed?
A — Accuracy: Is the information supported by evidence? Are claims cited?
P — Purpose: Why was it written? Is it objective or does it have a bias?

For academic work, prioritise: peer-reviewed journal articles, books from academic publishers, and reports from recognised institutions. Avoid Wikipedia as a primary source — it can be a starting point to find better references.

entries.q: How do I search the library catalogue?
entries.a:
To search the HCT Library catalogue:
1. Go to https://hct.primo.exlibrisgroup.com/discovery/jsearch?vid=971HCT_INST:971HCT_VU1
2. Enter keywords, author name, title, or ISBN
3. Use Advanced Search for more precise results: combine author + title + subject
4. Filter results by format (book, ebook, journal, DVD), location, or language
5. Check availability: green = available; red = checked out (you can place a hold)
6. For ebooks, click the access link — no borrowing needed

Tip: If you cannot find an item, try the Discovery Search which also searches across all library databases.

entries.q: How do I access ebooks?
entries.a:
HCT Library provides access to thousands of ebooks through multiple platforms:

1. ProQuest Ebook Central: Large academic ebook collection
2. EBSCOhost eBooks: Multidisciplinary ebook database
3. SpringerLink: Science, technology, and medicine ebooks
4. IEEE Xplore: Engineering and technology ebooks

How to access:
1. Search the library catalogue or discovery system
2. Click the ebook link in the record
3. If prompted off-campus, log in with your HCT credentials
4. Read online or download chapters for offline reading (download limits apply)

Most ebooks can be accessed on any device.

entries.q: What library workshops are available?
entries.a:
HCT Library offers regular training workshops throughout the semester. Current workshops include:

• Database Search Skills — learn effective searching in EBSCO, ProQuest
• APA 7th Edition Referencing — citations and reference lists
• Zotero/RefWorks Citation Management — organise your references
• Avoiding Plagiarism — academic integrity and paraphrasing
• Research Methods Introduction — starting your literature review
• Systematic Review Workshop — for postgraduate students

Workshops are free for all HCT students and staff. Sessions are held in the library training room. Check the library website for the current events calendar: https://hct.ac.ae/en/libraries/ — or contact library@hct.ac.ae to register.

entries.q: How do I connect to Wi-Fi in the library?
entries.a:
HCT Library provides high-speed Wi-Fi throughout all areas. To connect:
1. Select the network 'HCT-Student' (for students) or 'HCT-Staff' (for staff)
2. Log in with your HCT username and password
3. Alternatively, use the eduroam network if you are a visiting researcher from another institution

For laptop or device connection problems, visit the IT Help Desk located near the library entrance, or email IT support.

entries.q: What is plagiarism and how do I avoid it?
entries.a:
Plagiarism is presenting someone else's work, ideas, or words as your own without proper attribution. It is a serious academic offence at HCT.

Types of plagiarism:
• Direct copying without quotation marks and citation
• Paraphrasing too closely without citation
• Self-plagiarism: resubmitting your own previous work
• Collusion: sharing work with other students without permission

How to avoid plagiarism:
1. Always cite your sources using APA 7th edition
2. Use quotation marks for direct quotes
3. Paraphrase in your own words AND cite the source
4. Use citation management tools (Zotero, RefWorks)
5. Use Turnitin (via your course) to check your work before submission

HCT Library's subject and research guides are available at https://libguides.hct.ac.ae/?b=s

entries.q: Who is my subject librarian?
entries.a:
HCT Library has dedicated subject librarians for each academic area. Your subject librarian can help with:
• Tailored research consultations
• Database recommendations for your subject
• Collection development (requesting new resources)
• Embedding library instruction in your courses (for faculty)

To find your subject librarian:
1. Visit the library website: https://hct.ac.ae/en/libraries/
2. Browse subject guides at: https://libguides.hct.ac.ae/?b=s
3. Contact library@hct.ac.ae and we will connect you with the right specialist

You can also ask at the Reference Desk in person.

entries.q: Should I use Google Scholar for my research?
entries.a:
Google Scholar can be a useful starting point but has important limitations:

Advantages:
• Free, easy to use, covers many academic fields
• Shows citation counts (useful for finding influential papers)
• Links to library-subscribed full text via 'HCT Library' link

Limitations:
• Not comprehensive — misses many peer-reviewed articles
• Cannot filter results as precisely as library databases
• Includes non-peer-reviewed content
• Citation data can be inaccurate

Recommendation: Use Google Scholar to discover sources, then verify full text and peer-review status through library databases (EBSCO, ProQuest, Web of Science). For systematic literature reviews, use library databases rather than Google Scholar alone.

entries.q: How do I contact the library?
entries.a:
HCT Library contact information:

• Email: mosman@hct.ac.ae
• Phone: +971522087056 (Reception / Circulation)
• Live Chat: Available on the library website during operating hours
• In Person: Main campus library, Ground Floor, [Building Name]

For specific enquiries:
• Reference questions: reference@hct.ac.ae
• ILL requests: ill@hct.ac.ae
• Technical issues (database access): it.library@hct.ac.ae
• Library systems: systems.librarian@hct.ac.ae

Library hours: Sunday–Thursday 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM

entries.q: Can I borrow a laptop from the library?
entries.a:
Yes! HCT Library offers laptop lending for short-term in-library use.

Laptop lending rules:
• Available to current HCT students (valid HCT ID required)
• Loan period: up to 4 hours, renewable if not reserved by others
• For use within the library only — may not leave the premises
• A security deposit (or ID card held) may be required
• Limited number available — available on a first-come, first-served basis

To borrow: Present your HCT student ID at the circulation desk.

Charging stations for personal devices are available throughout the library — no borrowing required.

entries.q: What is course reserve and how do I access it?
entries.a:
Course Reserve is a special collection of textbooks and readings placed on short-term loan by your instructors.

Accessing course reserve:
1. Ask your instructor which textbooks are on reserve, or
2. Search the library catalogue by course code or instructor name
3. Physical reserve items: borrowed for 2 hours (in-library use) or 24 hours (overnight loan)
4. Electronic reserve: access directly online through the library portal — no borrowing needed

Note: Reserve items are in high demand. Plan ahead and do not rely on last-minute access during exam periods.
